meta.title: Fixed Posture Modules (Premium)
meta.description: Curated, fixed programs for premium users, separate from the rotating daily plan. Each is a hand-picked group of 4-6 exercises themed by time-of-day, body region, or context. All exercise_ids reference the 56-exercise Evidence-Based Posture Exercise Database v6. Every exercise is equipment-free (a wall or doorway is the only ambient surface used).
meta.ordering_principle: Within each program, exercises are sequenced to flow sensibly: mobility/warm-up first, then strengthening, then stretch or down-regulation, and positions are clustered to minimize getting up and down off the floor.
meta.design_notes: Programs intentionally reuse some exercises across themes because a fixed program is a self-contained product, unlike the rotating daily plan which avoids repeats. The daily-plan cooldown/family logic does NOT apply inside fixed programs. Module ids are stable and referenced by the icon map (types/Module.ts) and session messages (app/session.tsx); names/exercise lists may change but ids must not.
meta.revision_note: v6 rebuild: every program re-pointed to the current 56-exercise set after the database revision. rotation_flow repurposed from pure rotation (only one rotation exercise remains) into a full-plane Spine Mobility Flow. Each program's exercise set and description was made distinct from the others.

modules.name: Morning Unlock
modules.tagline: Gentle full-spine mobility to shake off overnight stiffness and stand tall.
modules.rationale: Mornings call for mobility over hard effort. This flow loosens the whole spine from the floor up, frees the shoulders and hips, and ends by consciously setting tall posture for the day.

modules.name: Desk Escape
modules.tagline: A quick standing and seated reset for the neck, upper back, and hips between meetings.
modules.rationale: The most useful office break needs no floor. It retracts the shoulder blades, resets the neck, opens the whole front of the body, lengthens the hip flexors that shorten in a chair, and closes with a screen-posture cue.

modules.name: Bedtime Release
modules.tagline: Long, slow holds and easy breathing to let the day go before sleep.
modules.rationale: Evenings favor down-regulation. Longer-hold neck and hip stretches plus a 90/90 breathing finish lower arousal rather than build strength, easing the body toward rest.

modules.name: Tech Neck Fix
modules.tagline: Strengthen the deep neck muscles and reset the screen-slump habit.
modules.rationale: Forward head posture responds best to deep cervical flexor strengthening. This loads the chin tuck with resistance and isometrics, releases the levator, and reinforces keeping the phone at eye level.

modules.name: Shoulder Un-Rounding
modules.tagline: Loosen the tight chest, then power up the shoulder-blade muscles that pull you back.
modules.rationale: Rounded shoulders need the front opened and the back strengthened. Open with a pec stretch, then drive serratus and scapular-retractor work, finishing on all fours for posterior tilt.

modules.name: Upright Upper Back
modules.tagline: Strengthen the spinal extensors that fight a hunched mid-back.
modules.rationale: Kyphosis improves most with extensor strengthening. After a cat-cow warm-up, prone cobra, superman, and swimmers load the thoracic erectors, then child's pose decompresses the spine.

modules.name: Lower Back & Core
modules.tagline: Train deep core control and pelvic position to take load off the low back.
modules.rationale: Low-back resilience comes from deep-core control, not crunches. Pelvic tilt and dead bug teach bracing, bird dog and the plank build endurance, and child's pose unloads the spine to close.

modules.name: Hip Flexor Reset
modules.tagline: Lengthen sitting-tight hip flexors and guide the pelvis back toward neutral.
modules.rationale: Anterior pelvic tilt eases when tight flexors lengthen and the pelvis re-learns neutral. Two flexor stretches and a posterior pelvic tilt set it up, a bridge anchors the glutes, and a figure-4 finishes.

modules.name: Gamer Reboot
modules.tagline: A four-region fix for the head-forward, shoulders-in, hips-locked gaming slump.
modules.rationale: Long sessions stack four faults at once, so this hits all of them in one pass: chin tuck for the neck, pec stretch and wall angel for the shoulders, a hip-flexor stretch and cobra to undo the seated lock.

modules.name: Full-Body Alignment
modules.tagline: One move per region, head to hips, for a complete posture tune-up.
modules.rationale: Modeled on the full-body routine every posture app offers. One exercise per region in head-to-hip order gives a balanced, complete tune-up in six moves.

modules.name: Seated Neck Relief
modules.tagline: Five chair-bound minutes to melt tension out of the neck and traps.
modules.rationale: A fully seated sequence for the upper traps, levator, and scalenes that shorten under forward head posture, bookended by a chin tuck and a shoulder-blade squeeze to restore upright carriage. No floor work.

modules.name: Chest & Spine Opener
modules.tagline: Reverse the forward collapse of sitting by opening the chest and extending the spine.
modules.rationale: Sitting locks the body in flexion. This pairs thoracic extension with two heights of pec stretch and a standing backbend to restore the opposite, extension-biased range of motion.

modules.name: Spine Mobility Flow
modules.tagline: Move the spine through the directions a desk makes it forget: flexion, extension, and rotation.
modules.rationale: The desk-bound spine loses rotation and extension first. This mobility flow takes it through every plane: cat-cow flexion and extension, thread-the-needle rotation, a child's pose decompression, then standing extension and a backbend.

modules.name: Glute Activation
modules.tagline: Wake up the glutes a chair switches off, from bridges to single-leg control.
modules.rationale: Prolonged sitting leaves the glutes inhibited. Bilateral and single-leg bridges, a clamshell, and prone hip extension fire all three glute heads before stretching their antagonist hip flexor.

modules.name: Shoulder Stability
modules.tagline: Drill the serratus and lower trap that keep the shoulder blade tracking cleanly.
modules.rationale: Clean shoulder mechanics depend on the serratus anterior and lower trapezius. Wall and floor slides groove the pattern, serratus presses and push-up-plus load it, and posterior tilt locks it in.

modules.name: Shoulder Blade Series
modules.tagline: The Y-T-W-I prone protocol that loads every fiber of the mid and lower traps.
modules.rationale: The Y-T-W-I protocol loads each region of the mid and lower trapezius in a shortened position, the most direct way to build the passive strength that holds the shoulders back all day. A scapular retraction caps it.

modules.name: Side Body Strength
modules.tagline: Build the side-body and rotator-cuff strength that resists sideways pelvic drop.
modules.rationale: The frontal-plane system gets neglected by front-facing training. External rotation and clamshells prime the hip and cuff, side plank and single-leg bridge load lateral stability, and standing bird dog integrates it upright.

modules.name: Deep Neck Protocol
modules.tagline: Four loaded chin-tuck variations plus extensor work for serious cervical strength.
modules.rationale: A progressive overload block for the longus colli and capitis. Wall and resisted chin tucks plus a gravity-loaded supine tuck build the deep flexors, then isometric and prone extension work balances the back of the neck.

modules.name: Dynamic Core
modules.tagline: Anti-rotation and anti-extension drills that protect the spine while you move.
modules.rationale: Built on McGill's anti-movement principle. Curl-up and side plank brace against flexion and lateral bend, bird dog and shoulder taps resist rotation, and standing bird dog carries the bracing into an upright pattern.

modules.name: Balance & Body Sense
modules.tagline: Single-leg and narrow-stance challenges that sharpen your sense of where you are.
modules.rationale: Proprioception drives the automatic postural reflexes that keep the spine stacked. Narrow-stance and single-leg work, a loaded single-leg bridge, and a closing posture check sharpen body awareness under fatigue.
